title: Listahan — Play Store Release
subtitle: Follow the blue “What you do” steps below each checkbox. Click Copy on any command, paste into PowerShell, press Enter.

steps.text: You need: a free Expo account, a Google Play Developer account ($25), and Listahan at C:\SayCart.

steps.label: 1. Open PowerShell and go to the project:

actions.text: AAB = Android App Bundle. Google Play requires this format (not APK).
Wait 10–20 minutes. When done, copy the build URL from the terminal into Sign-off at the bottom of this page.

items.label: EAS Android signing / keystore set up and backed up

actions.text: First production build usually asks to create a keystore. Say YES and let Expo store it.
Screenshot or save any keystore recovery info Expo shows. You need it to update the app forever.

items.label: versionName and versionCode correct for this release

actions.text: First release can stay 1.0.0 / versionCode 1. Every future Play upload must increase versionCode.
Open C:\SayCart\app.json — check "version" is 1.0.0 (or your chosen version).
Open C:\SayCart\android\app\build.gradle — check versionName and versionCode match.

items.label: Launcher icon and adaptive icon correct on a real device

actions.label: Regenerate icons (if you changed the logo):

actions.text: Rebuild the AAB after this, then install on your phone and check the app drawer icon.

items.label: Splash screen looks correct on cold start

actions.text: Install your production or preview build on a phone. Force-close the app, then open it again.
You should see the Listahan splash briefly, then the app — no blank screen or error.

items.label: App opens and completes bootstrap without Metro

actions.text: Release builds must NOT need "npx expo start" on your PC.
Turn off Wi‑Fi on your phone (or unplug USB). Open Listahan from the app drawer.
App should open to dashboard or username setup. If you see "Could not connect to Metro", rebuild the AAB/APK.
Sync/share need Supabase keys baked into the store build. Preview APK keys do NOT automatically apply to production.

actions.label: Check what is on production now:

actions.text: If EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL is missing, add it (see next item b-2 steps — same process for URL and anon key).
Get values from your .env file or Supabase dashboard → Project Settings → API.

actions.label: Add Supabase URL to production (replace YOUR_URL with real value):
actions.command: npx eas-cli env:create production --name EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL --value "YOUR_URL" --environment production --visibility plaintext --non-interactive --force

actions.label: Add anon key to production (replace YOUR_KEY):
actions.command: npx eas-cli env:create production --name EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY --value "YOUR_KEY" --environment production --visibility sensitive --non-interactive --force

actions.text: Skip unless you use OpenAI for bulk voice. Local voice parsing works without this key.

actions.label: Optional — add OpenAI key to production:
actions.command: npx eas-cli env:create production --name EXPO_PUBLIC_OPENAI_API_KEY --value "YOUR_KEY" --environment production --visibility sensitive --non-interactive --force

items.label: Rebuild AAB after any env change

actions.label: Run this in PowerShell after changing env vars:

items.label: Extend sync-eas-env script for production (not only preview)

actions.text: Today the script only syncs to preview. For production you used the eas-cli commands in b-2 above.
Ask your developer to update scripts/sync-eas-env-from-dotenv.mjs to also push to production — or keep using b-2 commands manually.
Google Play requires a web link — the in-app policy alone is not enough.
Host your policy on a public page (GitHub Pages, your website, Notion public page, etc.).
Copy that URL — you will paste it in Play Console → App content → Privacy policy.

items.label: Privacy policy matches actual app behavior

actions.text: Read the in-app policy: Listahan → Settings → Privacy policy.
Developer file to edit: C:\SayCart\src\constants\privacyPolicyContent.ts
Make sure hosted web policy matches what the app really does (sync, vault, camera, mic).

items.label: Remove placeholder footer about contact address before store release

actions.text: Edit privacyPolicyContent.ts — remove text: "We will add a dedicated contact address before store release."
Replace with your real support email.

items.label: Developer support email in Play Console

actions.text: Account details → Developer contact → add an email you check regularly.

items.label: Support email in privacy policy and in-app Settings

actions.text: Use the same email as Play Console everywhere (policy web page + privacyPolicyContent.ts).

items.label: Settings support flows wired (FAQ, Report, Feedback)

actions.text: Optional but recommended before public launch.
Settings → Report a problem / Feedback currently show placeholder alerts. Wire to mailto:your@email.com or a form.
Create app → name: Listahan → default language → App / Game: App → Free/Paid.
Package name must be: com.saycart.app (must match your AAB).
Play Console → Grow → Store presence → Main store listing → Short description.
Example: "Lists for groceries, to-dos, notes, reminders, and a secure vault."
Same page → Full description. Explain features: grocery lists, voice, sync, vault, reminders.
Export from C:\SayCart\assets\icon.png — resize to 512×512 PNG if needed, no transparency.
Create a banner image 1024×500 px for the store page header (Canva/Figma/etc.).
On your phone: open Listahan → take screenshots of dashboard, grocery list, vault, sync.
Upload under Main store listing → Phone screenshots.
Skip for v1 unless you test on a tablet.
Play Console → Store settings → App category → e.g. Productivity or Lifestyle.

items.label: Contact email and website filled in

actions.text: Store listing → Contact details → email (required). Website optional.

actions.label: Play Console → App content → Data safety:

actions.text: Declare: local list data, optional cloud profile (Supabase), optional sync, optional vault upload, camera, microphone, device ID for sync.
Say you do NOT sell data. Match your privacy policy exactly.
Play Console → App content → Content rating → Start questionnaire → answer honestly → apply rating.
Play Console → App content → Target audience. App is not directed at children under 13.

items.label: Ads declaration (none unless you add ads)

actions.text: Play Console → App content → Ads → No, my app does not contain ads.

items.label: News / COVID / government declarations if applicable

actions.text: Usually N/A for Listahan — skip unless Play Console asks.
Play Console → App content → Permission declarations. Reason: scan QR share codes / import from image.
Reason: voice entry for grocery items and bulk lists.
Reason: reminders and to-do timer chimes.
Play Console may ask about exact alarms. Reason: fire reminders at the exact time user chose.
Reason: optional fingerprint/Face unlock for Vault.
On Android 13+ phone: try importing a QR from gallery. Confirm it works or shows a clear permission prompt.
Standard — used for optional sync and cloud profile.
When app asks for camera/mic/notifications, the popup text should make sense. Test on device.

items.label: Play Console permission declarations match usage

actions.text: Do not declare permissions the app does not use. Match AndroidManifest.xml / app.json.

items.label: Graceful fallback when user denies permissions

actions.text: Deny camera → QR import should explain what's needed. Deny mic → voice buttons should not crash app.
Install production AAB from Play internal testing link. Open app fresh → pick username → continue.

items.label: Profile saves; greeting correct on dashboard

actions.text: Close and reopen app. Dashboard should say Hello, [your name] — not a flash of wrong text.

items.label: Privacy policy opens from Settings / Profile

actions.text: Settings → Privacy policy. Page should load without error.

items.label: Grocery: create, add, check off, complete, archive

actions.text: Create list → add items → check all → see Completed section → archive.
Create to-do list, add task, test timer/chime if you use them.
Notes tile → new note → save → reopen and edit.

items.label: Reminder fires at expected time (exact alarm)

actions.text: Set reminder 2 minutes from now. Lock phone. Wait — notification should appear on time.
Vault → set PIN → add entry → lock app or leave → unlock with PIN/biometrics.
Grocery list → Add item → tap mic → speak item name → confirm it appears.
Open + menu → Add bulk by Voice → say "one milk and two eggs" → check rows created.

items.label: Import code/QR on list detail sheet

actions.text: Inside a list → + → Import with Code/QR → paste code or scan QR.

items.label: Share import creates new list from code

actions.text: From home/share flow, import a share code → new list appears with items.
Dashboard or profile should show Sync if production Supabase keys were in the AAB.
Sync → enter partner's full tag (e.g. john_t1ci) → Look up.
Send request from phone A → accept on phone B (or second account).
After sync, lists on both phones should match selected tools.

items.label: Vault sync only when enabled + PIN confirmed

actions.text: Vault → Settings → Include Vault in sync → enter PIN → confirm sync includes vault only when opted in.

items.label: Dashboard tiles square; long descriptions scroll

actions.text: Vault tile description — swipe inside card to scroll if text is long.
Settings → Theme → Dark. Check dashboard and a list screen.

items.label: No greeting flash returning to dashboard

actions.text: Open Grocery → back to dashboard. Greeting should not flash "Welcome to Listahan".

items.label: Launcher icon is list+pen logo (not coral placeholder)

actions.text: Check app drawer — cream square with list + pen icon.
Airplane mode → open app → grocery/to-do still work for existing lists.
If keys missing, import/sync should say to configure Supabase — not crash.
Add items → force-close app from Android recents → reopen → data still there.

actions.label: Build AAB if you have not yet:

actions.text: Release → Testing → Internal testing → Create release → upload .aab from EAS download link.

items.label: Install from internal link; run section G tests

actions.text: Play Console gives a tester opt-in link. Open on your phone → install → work through section G checkboxes.
Testing → Closed testing → add testers by email → promote release from internal.
Play Console → Quality → Android vitals / Pre-launch report. Fix any crashes before production.
Production → Create release → use tested AAB → start with 10% rollout → increase to 100%.

sections.title: J. Known repo gaps (before public launch)

actions.text: Use section B commands until script is updated.
See section C item c-1.
See section C items c-4 and c-5.
See section C item c-3.

items.label: Settings FAQ / Report / Feedback — real contact

actions.text: See section C item c-6.
Complete all of section G on a production build.
